LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL HELTON'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT C.C.P. 473 B IS GRANTED. THE MOVING PAPERS PROVIDE SUFFICIENT GOOD CAUSE FOR THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION AND CALIFORNIA'S STRONG PUBLIC POLICY FAVORING RESOLUTION OF CASES ON THEIR MERITS ALSO SUPPORTS THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION. DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE WHICH MAY BE E-MAILED TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Motion In Support Of Prove Up Hearing Default Proceeding
Off Calendar Apr-19-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default C.C.P. 473 B
Off Calendar Mar-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JIM REILLY, ESQ. of Law Office of Jim Reilly, Tel No. 415-795-3957 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. GREGORY R. DE LA PENA, ESQ. of DE LA PENA & HOLIDAY LLP, Tel No. 415-268-8000 appearing for Defendant Michael Helton.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT MICHAEL HELTON'S Motion To Set Aside Default C.C.P. 473 B, Mr. Helton's motion to set aside the default is granted. The moving papers provide sufficient good cause for the granting of the motion and California's strong public policy favoring resolution of cases on their merits also supports the granting of the motion. Defendant shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature which may be e-mailed to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
